projectRatings.asset: JFK New Terminal One 2025 green bonds

debtIssuance.note: Short-term funding for treasury needs; managed by Corporate Treasury (David's desk).
KPI-linked (coupon step-ups tied to SBTi-aligned GHG targets), DNV second-party opinion. Not a use-of-proceeds green bond.
Tax-exempt project bonds: NTE ~$1.6bn, NTE 35W ~$1.6bn, LBJ ~$2.0bn, I-77 ~$0.47bn.

balanceSheet.comment: Net CASH at the corporate (recourse) level; the ~€5.9bn consolidated figure is almost entirely non-recourse project debt that does not contaminate the parent.

tsr.note: Total Shareholder Return = price appreciation + dividends reinvested. Ferrovial has materially outperformed European infra peers over 3y (re-rating + North-American toll growth). Shareholder remuneration FY2025: €657m (€501m buybacks + €156m cash dividend); ~€1bn proposed for 2026, ~€2.2bn aggregate 2024–2026.
tsr.buybackProgram: Up to €800m (max 15m shares, ~2.04%), Dec 2025 → Oct 2026, executed by Goldman Sachs.
tsr.dividend: Ferrovial Flexible Dividend (scrip): shareholders choose shares or cash. 2026 distribution ~€400m at €0.5578/share; 75.55% elected shares.
